meaning: n.草写小字adj.用草写小字写的,极小的
examples: The film was shot in 17 days, a minuscule amount of time.
This minuscule rotor wiggled clockwise or anticlockwise as molecules bumped into it from one side or the other.
Many of these have minuscule limbs; some look more like snakes than lizards and a few have completely lost the toes on their hind limbs.
Researchers didn't think they could test minuscule samples of materials and grasp their properties.
He pays little heed to the insistence that its intervention has been minimal, even minuscule.
